The alternative text is not sufficient to describe the individual icons and does not include the displayed text.
column11: In the "About This Map" and "Map Settings" sections, the programmatic heading levels do not logically match the visual heading presentation within the heading structure. The "About This Map" heading is an h5 while its subsections are h2s. A similar issue exists in the location popup when clicking on a node on the map, and in the initial modal that appears when loading the page. The buttons for "About this Map", "Location", and "Map Settings" at the top right do not have an accessible name. The checkbox switches in the "Map Settings" section do not have accessible names.

column30: Several elements do not show a visible indication of focus. Examples: elements in the popup that appears when clicking on a map node and the refresh button on the map.
column32:
The text for English/Español are not identified with lang attributes depending on the page language.
QC - the "lang" attribute is set at the page level.   There should be a starting lang="es" and and ending lang = "en" on the "Espanol" label on the English page and a starting lang="en" and and ending lang="es" on the Spanish page to have the slider control labels read correctly.

column873: Protect Your Family from Lead in Your Home (March 2021) (pdf)
column874: The brochure by the United States Environmental Protection Agency, Consumer Product Safety Commission, and Department of Housing and Urban Development outlines the risks of lead exposure from homes built before 1978, emphasizing the importance of testing for lead and following lead-safe practices during renovations to protect families, especially young children, from health hazards. It provides detailed guidance on identifying lead hazards, steps for reducing exposure, and resources for further information and assistance.
column911: The Map of Radon Zones was developed using data on indoor radon measurements, geology, aerial radioactivity, soil parameters, and foundation types. The EPA recommends that this map be supplemented with any available local data in order to further understand and predict the radon potential for a specific area.

column1096: "PFAS Explained" informs the public about the presence and risks associated with PFAS chemicals, which are widely used in various industries and consumer products, and their potential harmful health effects due to environmental exposure.
